name: baseline
description: unmodified inference, batch=8
notes: reference

name: baseline
description: unmodified inference, batch=8
notes: reference

name: baseline
description: unmodified inference, batch=8
notes: reference

name: tf32
description: torch.set_float32_matmul_precision(high) + tf32 enabled
notes: rejected: probability rtol > 1e-4

name: tf32
description: torch.set_float32_matmul_precision(high) + tf32 enabled
notes: rejected: probability rtol > 1e-4

name: baseline_final
description: unmodified inference at N=500, 2 runs (stability)
notes: v1=969.84 v2=975.62 v3=973.21 v4=973.27 ms/mol; equality stable run-to-run

name: translator_cleanup
description: hoist complete_seq_log_prob allocation; pre-transpose memory_bank once (avoid per-step .transpose+.contiguous); precompute memory_padding_mask; replace mask*x+~mask*y with torch.where
notes: primary translator-level win; pure refactor of attention setup

name: inference_mode
description: switch torch.no_grad to torch.inference_mode in translate_batch
notes: stacked on translator_cleanup; modest additional gain

name: decoder_src_pad_mask_hoist
description: skip per-step sequence_mask reconstruction in transformer_decoder.forward by accepting precomputed mask
notes: REVERTED in final stack: marginal gain (~0.2%), kept code simpler

name: persistent_mp_pool
description: replace per-call multiprocessing.Pool(4) with process-wide singleton pool
notes: rejected: introduces run-to-run nondeterminism in canonicalization (likely RDKit state in long-lived workers); not worth the equality risk

name: batch_size_16
description: increase batch size from 8 to 16 via config knob
notes: rejected: batch composition change perturbs SDPA reductions; prob delta ~2e-2

name: loc_batched_mm
description: concat all top-k rule LHS tensors into one mm + log_softmax + single CPU transfer
notes: rejected: cuBLAS picks different kernel for bigger GEMM; prob delta ~6e-5 just over rtol=1e-4

name: tf32_full
description: torch.set_float32_matmul_precision(high) + cudnn.allow_tf32=True
notes: rejected: TF32 reduces matmul precision; prob delta ~7e-4 > rtol; classic lossy speedup option

name: stacked_final
description: translator_cleanup + inference_mode (final state on opt/baseline)
notes: kept stack: -31% (N=32 ensemble), -33% (N=500 ensemble); peak mem essentially unchanged

name: stacked_final
description: translator_cleanup + inference_mode (final state on opt/baseline)
notes: transformer branch is the dominant cost; -34.7% latency

name: tf32_relaxed
description: torch.set_float32_matmul_precision(high) + cudnn.allow_tf32=True (re-tested under rtol=1e-3, >=99.9% pooled slot match)
notes: still rejected: probability deltas pass through softmax(score/T=3) amplification and exceed rtol on 1.6% of slots > 0.1% budget

name: loc_batched_mm_relaxed
description: concat top-k rule LHS into one mm + log_softmax + single CPU transfer (re-tested under rtol=1e-3, >=99.9% pooled slot match)
notes: accepted; committed as c775a17. Larger GEMM kernel produces sub-atol rounding deltas only.

name: blocked_gpu_nvml_unknown_error
description: GPU NVML init fails (Unknown Error) and CUDA driver returns NO_DEVICE (cuInit=100) ~30 min into session, after loc-batched-mm bench completed cleanly. /proc/driver/nvidia/gpus/0001:00:00.0 still shows A100 80GB present and /dev/nvidia* devices exist with 0666 perms; no process is holding them (fuser empty). Without root, cannot reset driver. Polled every 60s for 5 min; no recovery.
notes: blocked; preparing additional code changes meanwhile and will revalidate when GPU returns

name: resume_sanity
description: post-restart GPU sanity bench (warmup=2, repeat=3, N=32, B=8) on loc, transformer, ensemble; uncommitted edits stashed
notes: loc=30.54 ms (ref 30.4), transformer=616.43 ms (ref 615), ensemble=659.27 ms (ref 671). All within reference. cudnn warmup needed >=2 batches to reach steady state on loc.

name: threadpool_canonicalize
description: replace per-batch multiprocessing.Pool(4) for canonicalize_smiles_clear_map with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(4) (inline if <=64 items)
notes: rejected: SLOWER than mp.Pool baseline (616 -> 729 ms, +18%). Likely RDKit canonicalization is GIL-bound enough that 4 threads serialize, and the threshold-or-inline branch made the small-N case purely serial. Discarding.

name: sdpa_fast_path
description: set self_attn_type='scaled-dot-flash' on every MHA module to route attention through torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention (mem_efficient backend)
notes: rejected: BOTH slower (616 -> 722 ms, +17%) and produces wildly different outputs (89% slot mismatch). SDPA-mem-efficient kernel uses different masking semantics or numerical path than the manual matmul/softmax for this model's mask convention.

name: beam_cpu_snapshot
description: in BeamSearch.update_finished, snapshot predictions+topk_scores to CPU once per call so the per-(batch,beam) loop's `predictions[i,j,-2] != self.eos` check and the `sorted(hypotheses, key=score)` step stop forcing per-element .item() syncs
notes: accepted: 616.43 -> 482.21 ms (-22% on stack, -49% vs original baseline). Profile showed ~220k aten::_local_scalar_dense calls; this collapses them to one DtoH per call.

name: beam_cpu_snapshot
description: same change, validated on full ensemble
notes: accepted: 659.27 -> 526.73 ms (-20% on stack, -46% vs original).

name: beam_cpu_snapshot_n500
description: promote beam_cpu_snapshot to N=500 final bench against original baseline_final_ensemble.json
notes: promoted: 969.84 -> 506.00 ms/mol (-47.8% vs original, -22.3% on prior stack at 651.5)

name: decoder_padmask_shortcut
description: in TransformerDecoder.forward, skip the eq(0)+sum+long+sequence_mask(src_len,src_max_len) round-trip and just unsqueeze the already-boolean enc_key_padding_mask; also removes the implicit lengths.max() sync from sequence_mask's assert
notes: accepted: 482.21 -> 474.17 ms (-1.7% on stack). Removes ~5 small kernels + 1 sync per decode step.

name: store_cpu_predictions
description: store predictions_cpu (already snapshotted) in self.hypotheses/self.predictions instead of the GPU view, so the per-beam .tolist() during tokenizer.decode() at output time stops triggering 1k+ DtoH syncs
notes: accepted: 474.17 -> 468.48 ms (-1.2% on stack); -10MB peak mem because GPU predictions can be freed earlier. Reuses the CPU snapshot we already have.

name: tf32_loc_relaxed
description: torch.set_float32_matmul_precision(high)+cudnn.allow_tf32=True restricted to LOC branch via RC_TF32=1 env; tested under rtol=1e-3, slot match >=99.9%
notes: rejected: SMILES all match (rules pick same), but TF32 GEMM noise -> softmax over rule scores moves 48/320 probabilities by up to 2.6% rel (>>1e-3 budget). Marginal speedup (29.66 vs 30.36 baseline, ~2.3%) on top of loc_batched_mm; not worth it.

name: bf16_autocast_transformer
description: wrap translator.translate_batch in torch.autocast(device_type='cuda', dtype=bfloat16); gated by RC_AUTOCAST=bf16
notes: rejected for equality (84/320 slot mismatches, 41 SMILES differ + 43 prob-only). Tempting perf: -12% on stack and -32% peak mem. Code/env-var gating left in place (no-op when RC_AUTOCAST unset) for future experiments with selective fp32 islands.

name: full_stack_ensemble_n500
description: promotion run with all current opt/baseline commits (beam_cpu_snapshot + decoder_padmask + store_cpu_predictions + RC_AUTOCAST gate off)
notes: current accepted stack: 969.84 -> 498.62 ms/mol on N=500 ensemble (-48.6%).

name: ensemble_streams
description: run loc+transformer on separate CUDA streams via ThreadPoolExecutor inside RetroChimeraModel._get_reactions
notes: ACCEPTED. Quick: 494.77 ms (-6% vs serial stack). N=500: 470.24 ms (-51.5% vs baseline 969.84). Peak mem 8476->9252 MB (+9%) from concurrent working set. 100% slot match.

name: bf16_encoder_only_transformer
description: wrap _run_encoder in autocast bf16 only (decoder + token_fc stay fp32); cast memory_bank back to fp32 on exit; gated by RC_AUTOCAST_ENCODER
notes: rejected: still 32/320 mismatches (15 SMILES diff + 17 prob), and almost no speedup (471 vs 468). Encoder is a small fraction of total time; bf16 of cross-attention keys propagates beyond what the decoder fp32 cast can recover. Reverted.

name: sequential_canonicalize
description: skip mp.Pool(4) when len(lines)<=2000 and run canonicalize_smiles_clear_map serially
notes: rejected: serial RDKit canonicalize is +120 ms/batch slower than mp.Pool(4) (590 vs 468). Pool fork overhead is not the bottleneck; the work itself is. mp.Pool(4) stays.

name: baseline_b1
description: pre-opt baseline ec39be3 at batch_size=1, N=32
notes: B=1 reference. Reference B=8 baseline was 41.1 ms/mol; per-mol cost ~1.54x at B=1.

name: baseline_b1
description: pre-opt baseline ec39be3 at batch_size=1, N=32
notes: B=1 reference. B=8 baseline was 942.1 ms/mol; per-mol cost ~2.05x at B=1 because beam-search step is fixed-size.

name: baseline_b1
description: pre-opt baseline ec39be3 at batch_size=1, N=32 and N=500
notes: B=1 reference for all future experiments. B=8 baseline was 969.84 ms/mol at N=500.

name: stacked_final_b1
description: current opt/baseline HEAD bcf91ec at batch_size=1, N=32
notes: stacked: 63.42 -> 56.61 ms (-10.7%). Compare B=8: 41.1 -> 30.5 (-25.7%). loc gains shrink at B=1 because the GEMM gets too small.

name: stacked_final_b1
description: current opt/baseline HEAD bcf91ec at batch_size=1, N=32
notes: stacked: 1934.95 -> 1316.83 ms (-31.9%). Compare B=8: 942 -> 468 (-50.3%). Sync-elimination wins still significant; absolute work-per-mol is still beam_size*decode_steps so per-call overhead amortized over more decode-step kernels.

name: stacked_final_b1
description: current opt/baseline HEAD bcf91ec at batch_size=1, N=32 and N=500
notes: stacked: 2071.65 -> 1386.80 ms quick, 2027.56 -> 1347.16 ms N=500 (-33.6%). Compare B=8: -51.5%. Peak mem +16.7% (vs +9.1% at B=8) - ensemble_streams concurrent working set is a bigger fraction of total at B=1.

name: skip_redundant_update_finished
description: in BeamSearch.update_finished, track already-appended beams on CPU and early-return when no NEW beam finished this step
notes: REJECTED. Unsafe: in customised_beam_search mode, batches where is_finished[i].all() but len(hypotheses[b])<n_best (common because per-beam append is gated on predictions[i,j,-2]!=eos) stop terminating once early-return fires — no new beam can finish, so update_finished is skipped forever and decode runs to max_length. Result: +413% latency, 93% slot mismatch. Dropped branch.

name: prealloc_kv_cache_b1
description: pre-allocate self-attn KV cache in chunks of 32 + in-place copy_ at step index, replacing per-step torch.cat
notes: REJECTED. Decode hung (>240s for 5 single-mol calls at B=1, ~50x slowdown). Hypothesis: slicing buf[:, :, :cache_len, :] returns a non-contiguous view (batch dim has gaps from the unused chunk tail), and torch.matmul + transpose on that view either triggers internal .contiguous() copies that dominate, or upstream ops misbehave on the strided view, sending outputs astray (no EOS -> decode runs to max_length). Reverted. The torch.cat path stays.
